name: Mas'ud ibn Hajjaj
title: Taymi Brave
shortDescription: A brave man from Kufa who came to Karbala with his son in Umar Sa'd's army, but repented and joined Imam Hussain (AS).
fullStory:
Mas'ud ibn Hajjaj al-Taymi was a brave, well-known man and a Shia of Kufa. Initially, to protect their lives and under compulsion, he and his son (Abdurrahman) left Kufa with Umar Sa'd's army and came to Karbala.

Mas'ud, who held the love of the Ahl al-Bayt (AS) in his heart, was looking for an opportunity to defect from the army of falsehood. In the days before Ashura (seventh or eighth of Muharram), he and his son left Umar Sa'd's camp under some pretext and reached Imam Hussain's (AS) tents.

The Imam accepted their presence, and Mas'ud remained at the side of the Master of Martyrs. On the day of Ashura, with the start of the arrow barrage and the brutal all-out attack by the Kufans (the first attack), Mas'ud ibn Hajjaj valiantly entered the battlefield.

He fought with his sword shoulder to shoulder with his son and the other companions of the Imam, and after a manifest battle, he attained the great honor of martyrdom in that very first attack, and his name was recorded among the saved.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى مَسْعُودِ بْنِ الحَجّاجِ وَابْنِهِ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Mas'ud ibn Hajjaj and his son.
